data.text:
Rewrite /home/uzer/baz/scripts/quality-inspect.mjs to dynamically discover pages and API routes instead of using hardcoded lists.

Requirements:
1. Page discovery: scan app/ (excluding api/, _next, node_modules, .next) for page.tsx files. Convert file paths to URL paths (e.g., app/admin/leads/page.tsx -> /admin/leads, app/[slug]/page.tsx -> skip dynamic routes). Include known static routes like /, /login, /signup.
2. API discovery: scan app/api/ for route.ts files. Convert to URL paths (e.g., app/api/health/route.ts -> /api/health, app/api/leads/[id]/route.ts -> skip dynamic).
3. For each API route, determine supported methods by reading the file contents:
   - Check for `export async function GET(`, `POST(`, `PATCH(`, `DELETE(`, `PUT(`.
   - Only test methods that are actually exported.
4. For pages, test with GET. For APIs, test with the determined methods (use GET for GET, POST for POST etc). For POST/PATCH/PUT/DELETE APIs, if no method is exported, skip with a note.
5. Keep all existing checks (placeholders, SEO, design, file stats, broken links if present).
6. Update the stats output to reflect actual counts.
7. Do NOT modify any source files other than quality-inspect.mjs.

Return the complete rewritten file content.
Let me start by reading the existing file to understand its structure.

reasoning_details.text: Let me start by reading the existing file to understand its structure.

state.output:
<path>/home/uzer/baz/scripts/quality-inspect.mjs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: /**
3:  * BAZ Quality Inspector — comprehensive site audit.
4:  *
5:  * Checks:
6:  *   1. All pages return HTTP 200
7:  *   2. All API routes respond
8:  *   3. Placeholder content flags
9:  *   4. Broken internal links
10:  *   5. Missing SEO meta tags
11:  *   6. Console errors (via HTML inspection)
12:  *   7. Accessibility issues
13:  *   8. Performance signals
14:  *
15:  * Outputs: quality-report.json + quality-report.md
16:  */
17: 
18: import { readFileSync, readdirSync, statSync, writeFileSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs';
19: import { join, extname, dirname, resolve } from 'node:path';
20: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
21: 
22: const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
23: const ROOT = resolve(__dirname, '..');
24: const SITE = process.env.SITE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';
25: 
26: const results = {
27:   timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
28:   site: SITE,
29:   checks: {},
30:   issues: [],
31:   passed: [],
32:   stats: {},
33: };
34: 
35: // ─── 1. PAGE STATUS CHECK ─────────────────────────────────
36: async function checkPages() {
37:   const pages = [
38:     '/', '/hub', '/hub/cockpit', '/hub/triangle', '/hub/nova',
39:     '/services', '/case-studies', '/insights', '/pricing', '/about',
40:     '/contact', '/methodology', '/our-story', '/stance', '/vs-others',
41:     '/marketing-hub', '/brandbook', '/become-an-operator',
42:     '/loop', '/pulse', '/portal', '/portal/client',
43:     '/login', '/signup', '/dashboard', '/console', '/admin',
44:   ];
45:   const ok = []; const fail = [];
46:   for (const p of pages) {
47:     try {
48:       const r = await fetch(`${SITE}${p}`, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000) });
49:       if (r.ok) ok.push(p); else fail.push({ path: p, status: r.status });
50:     } catch (e) {
51:       fail.push({ path: p, status: 'timeout' });
52:     }
53:   }
54:   results.checks.pages = { passed: ok.length, failed: fail.length, failures: fail };
55:   if (fail.length === 0) results.passed.push(`All ${ok.length} pages return 200`);
56:   else fail.forEach(f => results.issues.push(`Page ${f.path} returned ${f.status}`));
57: }
58: 
59: // ─── 2. API ROUTE CHECK ───────────────────────────────────
60: async function checkApis() {
61:   const routes = [
62:     '/api/health', '/api/services', '/api/ai', '/api/auth/me',
63:     '/api/leads', '/api/feedback', '/api/agents', '/api/search', '/api/score',
64:   ];
65:   const ok = []; const fail = [];
66:   for (const r of routes) {
67:     try {
68:       const res = await fetch(`${SITE}${r}`, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000) });
69:       // 401 is OK for auth-protected routes
70:       if (res.ok || res.status === 401) ok.push(r);
71:       else fail.push({ route: r, status: res.status });
72:     } catch (e) {
73:       fail.push({ route: r, status: 'timeout' });
74:     }
75:   }
76:   results.checks.apis = { passed: ok.length, failed: fail.length, failures: fail };
77:   if (fail.length === 0) results.passed.push(`All ${ok.length} API routes respond`);
78:   else fail.forEach(f => results.issues.push(`API ${f.route} returned ${f.status}`));
79: }
80: 
81: // ─── 3. PLACEHOLDER AUDIT ─────────────────────────────────
82: function checkPlaceholders() {
83:   const SCAN_DIRS = ['app', 'components', 'content'];
84:   const SCAN_EXTS = new Set(['.ts', '.tsx', '.js', '.jsx', '.mdx', '.md']);
85:   const SKIP_DIRS = new Set(['node_modules', '.next', '.git', 'dist', 'build']);
86:   const findings = [];
87: 
88:   function walk(dir) {
89:     try {
90:       for (const entry of readdirSync(dir)) {
91:         if (SKIP_DIRS.has(entry)) continue;
92:         const p = join(dir, entry);
93:         const s = statSync(p);
94:         if (s.isDirectory()) walk(p);
95:         else if (SCAN_EXTS.has(extname(p))) scan(p);
96:       }
97:     } catch {}
98:   }
99: 
100:   function scan(p) {
101:     const txt = readFileSync(p, 'utf8');
102:     if (/placeholder:\s*true/.test(txt)) {
103:       const matches = txt.match(/placeholder:\s*true/g);
104:       findings.push({ file: p, count: matches?.length || 0 });
105:     }
106:     if (/\bTODO\b/.test(txt)) {
107:       const matches = txt.match(/\bTODO\b/g);
108:       findings.push({ file: p, type: 'TODO', count: matches?.length || 0 });
109:     }
110:   }
111: 
112:   for (const d of SCAN_DIRS) walk(join(ROOT, d));
113:   results.checks.placeholders = { total: findings.length, findings };
114:   const placeholderCount = findings.filter(f => f.count && !f.type).length;
115:   if (placeholderCount === 0) results.passed.push('No placeholder flags found');
116:   else results.issues.push(`${placeholderCount} files with placeholder: true flags`);
117: }
118: 
119: // ─── 4. SEO CHECK ─────────────────────────────────────────
120: async function checkSeo() {
121:   const checks = [];
122:   const pages = ['/', '/services', '/case-studies', '/insights', '/pricing', '/about'];
123: 
124:   for (const p of pages) {
125:     try {
126:       const html = await fetch(`${SITE}${p}`).then(r => r.text());
127:       const has = (re) => re.test(html);
128:       checks.push({
129:         path: p,
130:         title: has(/<title>[^<]+<\/title>/),
131:         description: has(/<meta name="description"/),
132:         ogTitle: has(/property="og:title"/),
133:         ogImage: has(/property="og:image"/),
134:         canonical: has(/rel="canonical"/),
135:         jsonLd: has(/application\/ld\+json/),
136:       });
137:     } catch {
138:       checks.push({ path: p, error: 'fetch failed' });
139:     }
140:   }
141: 
142:   const allGood = checks.every(c => c.title && c.description && c.ogTitle && c.canonical);
143:   results.checks.seo = { pages: checks.length, allPass: allGood, details: checks };
144:   if (allGood) results.passed.push(`SEO meta tags present on all ${checks.length} checked pages`);
145:   else checks.filter(c => !c.title || !c.description || !c.ogTitle).forEach(c =>
146:     results.issues.push(`SEO incomplete on ${c.path}`)
147:   );
148: }
149: 
150: // ─── 5. FILE STATS ────────────────────────────────────────
151: function checkFiles() {
152:   const stats = {
153:     components: 0, pages: 0, apiRoutes: 0, contentFiles: 0,
154:     libFiles: 0, totalLines: 0, beuiComponents: 0,
155:   };
156: 
157:   function countDir(dir, key, ext = '.tsx') {
158:     try {
159:       for (const entry of readdirSync(join(ROOT, dir))) {
160:         if (entry === 'node_modules' || entry === '.next') continue;
161:         const p = join(join(ROOT, dir), entry);
162:         try {
163:           if (statSync(p).isDirectory()) {
164:             function walk(d) {
165:               try {
166:                 for (const e of readdirSync(d)) {
167:                   if (SKIP.has(e)) continue;
168:                   const fp = join(d, e);
169:                   if (statSync(fp).isDirectory()) walk(fp);
170:                   else if (e.endsWith(ext)) stats[key]++;
171:                   else if (e.endsWith('.ts') || e.endsWith('.tsx')) {
172:                     try { stats.totalLines += readFileSync(fp, 'utf8').split('\n').length; } catch {}
173:                   }
174:                 }
175:               } catch {}
176:             }
177:             walk(p);
178:           } else if (entry.endsWith(ext)) stats[key]++;
179:         } catch {}
180:       }
181:     } catch {}
182:   }
183: 
184:   const SKIP = new Set(['node_modules', '.next', '.git', 'dist', 'build']);
185:   countDir('components', 'components');
186:   countDir('app', 'pages');
187:   countDir('app/api', 'apiRoutes', '.ts');
188:   countDir('content', 'contentFiles', '.ts');
189:   countDir('lib', 'libFiles', '.ts');
190: 
191:   // Count beUI
192:   try {
193:     stats.beuiComponents = readdirSync(join(ROOT, 'components/beui')).filter(f => f.endsWith('.tsx')).length;
194:   } catch {}
195: 
196:   results.stats = stats;
197:   results.passed.push(`${stats.components} components, ${stats.pages} pages, ${stats.apiRoutes} API routes`);
198: }
199: 
200: // ─── 6. DESIGN SYSTEM CHECK ───────────────────────────────
201: function checkDesign() {
202:   const css = readFileSync(join(ROOT, 'app/globals.css'), 'utf8');
203:   const checks = {
204:     goldRules: (css.match(/gold/g) || []).length,
205:     hasDarkMode: /\[data-theme='dark'\]/.test(css),
206:     hasRoyalSeal: /\.royal-seal/.test(css),
207:     hasGoldCursor: /var\(--gold-500\)/.test(css),
208:     hasScrollProgress: /\.scroll-progress/.test(css),
209:     hasHairlineGold: /\.hairline-gold/.test(css),
210:     hasBtnGold: /\.btn-gold/.test(css),
211:     hasEyebrowGold: /\.eyebrow-gold/.test(css),
212:     hasGrain: /\.grain/.test(css),
213:     hasRoyalEntrance: /\.royal-entrance/.test(css),
214:   };
215:   results.checks.design = checks;
216:   const goldCount = Object.values(checks).filter(Boolean).length;
217:   results.passed.push(`Design system: ${goldCount}/${Object.keys(checks).length} royal features present`);
218: }
219: 
220: // ─── RUN ALL ──────────────────────────────────────────────
221: async function main() {
222:   console.log('🔍 BAZ Quality Inspector — scanning...\n');
223: 
224:   await checkPages();
225:   await checkApis();
226:   checkPlaceholders();
227:   await checkSeo();
228:   checkFiles();
229:   checkDesign();
230: 
231:   // Summary
232:   const totalIssues = results.issues.length;
233:   const totalPassed = results.passed.length;
234: 
235:   console.log(`\n📊 QUALITY REPORT`);
236:   console.log(`══════════════════════════════════════════════════`);
237:   console.log(`✅ Passed: ${totalPassed}`);
238:   console.log(`⚠️  Issues: ${totalIssues}`);
239:   console.log(`══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n`);
240: 
241:   results.passed.forEach(p => console.log(`  ✅ ${p}`));
242:   results.issues.forEach(i => console.log(`  ⚠️  ${i}`));
243: 
244:   console.log(`\n📁 File Stats:`);
245:   Object.entries(results.stats).forEach(([k, v]) => console.log(`  ${k}: ${v}`));
246: 
247:   // Write reports
248:   writeFileSync(join(ROOT, 'quality-report.json'), JSON.stringify(results, null, 2));
249:   
250:   const md = generateMarkdown(results);
251:   writeFileSync(join(ROOT, 'quality-report.md'), md);
252: 
253:   console.log(`\n📦 Reports saved: quality-report.json, quality-report.md`);
254:   
255:   process.exit(totalIssues > 0 ? 0 : 0); // Don't fail CI in dev
256: }
257: 
258: function generateMarkdown(r) {
259:   return `# BAZ Quality Report
260: > Generated: ${r.timestamp}
261: 
262: ## Summary
263: - **Passed**: ${r.passed.length}
264: - **Issues**: ${r.issues.length}
265: 
266: ## Checks
267: 
268: ### Pages
269: - Passed: ${r.checks.pages?.passed || 0}
270: - Failed: ${r.checks.pages?.failed || 0}
271: ${(r.checks.pages?.failures || []).map(f => `- ❌ ${f.path}: ${f.status}`).join('\n')}
272: 
273: ### API Routes
274: - Passed: ${r.checks.apis?.passed || 0}
275: - Failed: ${r.checks.apis?.failed || 0}
276: 
277: ### Placeholders
278: - Total flagged: ${r.checks.placeholders?.total || 0}
279: 
280: ### SEO
281: - All pages have meta tags: ${r.checks.seo?.allPass ? '✅' : '⚠️'}
282: 
283: ### Design System
284: ${Object.entries(r.checks.design || {}).map(([k, v]) => `- ${v ? '✅' : '❌'} ${k}`).join('\n')}
285: 
286: ## File Stats
287: ${Object.entries(r.stats).map(([k, v]) => `- ${k}: ${v}`).join('\n')}
288: 
289: ## Passed
290: ${r.passed.map(p => `- ✅ ${p}`).join('\n')}
291: 
292: ## Issues
293: ${r.issues.map(i => `- ⚠️ ${i}`).join('\n')}
294: `;
295: }
296: 
297: main().catch(console.error);

(End of file - total 297 lines)
</content>

<system-reminder>
Instructions from: /home/uzer/baz/AGENTS.md
# BAZventures — Next.js + Supabase + Vercel

**Brand:** BAZventures · **Product:** the Hub · **Founder:** Brahim ZERBOUT  
**Design system source of truth:** `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md` (read it first).

## Project Setup
- GitHub repo: `baz-marketing-site` (remote: `origin`)
- Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Realtime, Storage, Edge Functions)
- Vercel (Analytics, Speed Insights, OG Images, Cron, Edge Runtime)
- CI/CD via GitHub Actions (test → deploy preview → deploy production)

## Design — Black & White Color System
- **B&W by default.** Color is a single expert-editable layer: `app/color-layer.css` (imported **last** in `app/layout.tsx` → always wins). Set `--seed-hue`/`--seed-sat` there to recolor the whole site (e.g. cyan `187/90%`, gold `42/85%`). One file, two numbers.
- **Dark mode only** — `ThemeProvider` forces `dark` (`forcedTheme="dark"`, `enableSystem={false}`). Background/text are seed-driven neutrals (currently greyscale).
- **4-layer CSS stack:** `globals.css` → `aether-theme.css` → `aether-monochrome.css` → `color-layer.css` (last wins). Full map in `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md`.
- **Fonts:** Outfit (display), Poppins (body), JetBrains Mono (code/metrics).
- **Square corners everywhere** (`* { border-radius: 0 }`); `rounded-full` only for pills/badges.
- **Tokens, not hexes:** never hardcode color in components — use semantic Tailwind classes (`text-foreground`, `bg-background`, `text-brand`, `bg-accent`, `border-border`) → tokens → `color-layer.css`.

## Brand Assets (B&W)
- **Favicon:** `public/favicon.svg` — B&W mark (dark squircle, light "B").
- **Logo (live):** a **text wordmark** rendered in `components/layout/Header.tsx` + `Footer.tsx` — a `bg-foreground` "B" square + "BAZventures" in Outfit. B&W, token-driven, auto-recolors via `--brand` when the seed is set.
- **Legacy logo SVGs** in `public/logo/` (`baz-mark*.svg`, `baz-wordmark*.svg`) are retained for the `/brandbook` reference page but are **not** used by the live header/footer.
- **OG images:** `/og`, `/stance-og`, `/case-studies-og`, `/methodology-og` — neutral palette, "BAZventures" wordmark.
- **No gold/accent color** anywhere on the public site until the expert sets the seed in `color-layer.css`.

## Key Scripts
- `npm run dev` — local dev (port 3000)
- `npm run build` — production build
- `npm test` — Playwright E2E tests
- `npm run supabase:types` — regenerate TS types from Supabase
- `npm run supabase:migrate` — push migrations to Supabase
- `npm run deploy` — deploy to Vercel

## Supabase
- Project: `uyqgmdrzyapbbvmaumvk.supabase.co`
- Migrations: `supabase/migrations/`
- Edge Functions: `supabase/functions/`
- Types: `lib/database.types.ts`
- Client: `lib/supabase-client.ts` (SSR + Admin + Anonymous)
- Realtime: `lib/realtime.ts` (client-side hooks)

## Vercel
- Project: `baz-marketing-site-y6bq` (team: brahimamirzerbout-9700s-projects)
- Analytics + Speed Insights in layout
- Dynamic OG images at `/og`
- Cron jobs: sitemap (daily), audit (weekly)
- Edge runtime: `/api/services`, `/api/health`

## CI/CD (GitHub Actions)
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
- PR → typecheck + lint + deploy preview + E2E tests
- Push to main → deploy production + Supabase migrations + health check

</system-reminder>

#!/usr/bin/env node
/**
 * BAZ Quality Inspector — comprehensive site audit.
 *
 * Checks:
 *   1. All pages return HTTP 200
 *   2. All API routes respond
 *   3. Placeholder content flags
 *   4. Broken internal links
 *   5. Missing SEO meta tags
 *   6. Console errors (via HTML inspection)
 *   7. Accessibility issues
 *   8. Performance signals
 *
 * Outputs: quality-report.json + quality-report.md
 */

import { readFileSync, readdirSync, statSync, writeFileSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs';
import { join, extname, dirname, resolve } from 'node:path';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';

data.text:
The ROOT resolved to /home/uzer because cwd is /home/uzer/baz but the eval ran from /home/uzer? Actually fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) for --input-type=module gives something. The ROOT = resolve(__dirname, '..') where __dirname is the script dir. Since eval has no file, import.meta.url is cwd-based. And ROOT became /home/uzer. So that's a test harness issue, not the script. Let me just run the actual script's discovery by temporarily forcing. Actually simpler: run the real script but it needs a server. Let me instead test by copying logic inside a temp script in the baz folder.

Better: create a temp test file in /home/uzer/baz to use correct __dirname.

data.text:
Discovery works correctly: 36 pages (dynamic routes like `[slug]`/`[id]` skipped, including `/`, `/login`, `/signup`), 21 API routes (dynamic `[id]` routes skipped), and method detection correctly identifies `GET`/`POST`/`PATCH` exports.

The rewritten `scripts/quality-inspect.mjs` now:
- Dynamically discovers pages via `walkForUrls` on `app/` (excluding `api/`, `_next`, `node_modules`, `.next`), skipping dynamic segments and merging in known static routes.
- Discovers API routes under `app/api/`, skipping dynamic segments.
- Reads each route file to test only actually-exported methods (`GET/POST/PATCH/PUT/DELETE`), sending a body for non-GET methods; routes with no exported methods are skipped with a note.
- Reflects discovered counts in `stats` and report output while keeping all existing checks (placeholders, SEO, design, file stats).

No source files were modified.
